title: Ledgerise Canonical Transaction
description: The normalized internal transaction record produced by all inbound adapters and consumed by the journal engine. Every inbound adapter must map its source data to this schema. The journal engine only speaks this format.

id.description: Globally unique identifier for this transaction record within Ledgerise. Generated by the inbound adapter at normalization time. Not the source system's transaction ID.

source_id.description: The original transaction identifier from the source system (e.g. payment processor reference, POS receipt number). Preserved for traceability and deduplication. Required when available.

source.description: Identifies where this transaction originated. Used for deduplication and audit trail.

adapter.description: The inbound adapter that produced this record. Use lowercase kebab-case. e.g. 'zoho-webhook', 'csv-import', 'paystack-api'.

system.description: The upstream system or platform. e.g. 'paystack', 'flutterwave', 'interswitch', 'internal-pos'.

environment.description: Whether this transaction originated from a live or test/sandbox environment. Test transactions must never be posted to the accounting system.

occurred_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the transaction event occurred in the source system. This is the business date used for journal entry dating.

settled_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the transaction was settled or finalized. Null if the transaction is still pending. The journal engine only processes records where settled_at is not null.

processed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when Ledgerise normalized this record. Set by the inbound adapter.

status.description: The settlement status of the transaction. Only 'settled' transactions are eligible for journal posting. 'reversed' transactions trigger reversal journal entries if already posted.

type.description: The business type of the transaction using dot notation (category.subcategory). Adapters must normalize source-specific types to one of the standard enum values below where possible. For operator-specific edge cases not covered by the standard taxonomy, custom types are permitted provided they follow dot notation convention (e.g. 'payment.toll', 'agency.crop-insurance') AND a corresponding mapping rule exists in the operator's mapping configuration. The engine will flag any type, standard or custom, that has no matching mapping rule as 'unmapped' and hold it for manual review.

oneOf.description: Custom operator-defined type. Must follow dot notation: category.subcategory or category.subcategory.detail. A matching mapping rule must exist in the operator mapping configuration or the transaction will be held as unmapped.

direction.description: Direction of the transaction from the operator's perspective. 'debit' means funds left the operator's float or wallet. 'credit' means funds entered.

amount.description: Transaction amount in the smallest unit of the currency (e.g. kobo for NGN, cents for USD). Always a positive number. Direction is captured in the 'direction' field, not by sign.

fee.description: Fee breakdown for this transaction, if applicable. Fees are expressed in the same currency as the transaction.

platform_fee.description: Fee charged by the operator to the customer or merchant. Represents revenue to the operator.

processing_fee.description: Fee charged by an upstream aggregator or payment rail to the operator. Represents a cost to the operator.

net_fee.description: platform_fee minus processing_fee. Represents the operator's margin on this transaction.

principal.description: Identifies the customer, merchant, or agent who initiated or is the subject of this transaction.

id.description: The operator's internal identifier for the principal. Required. Must not contain PII directly.

type.description: The class of principal. 'internal' is used for system-generated entries such as settlement sweeps.

reference.description: A human-readable reference for the principal e.g. phone number (masked), account number, or agent code. Optional. Must be masked if sensitive.

channel.description: The payment channel through which the transaction was initiated.

product.description: Identifies the operator's product line and the biller or counterparty involved. This is the primary input to the mapping engine for determining which COA accounts to debit and credit.

line.description: The operator's product line. e.g. 'consumer-app', 'api', 'pos', 'gateway', 'ussd'. Defined by the operator in mapping configuration.

biller.description: The biller, merchant, or counterparty identifier. e.g. 'ikeja-electric', 'mtn', 'dstv', 'buypower'. Used with product.line to resolve mapping rules.

biller_category.description: The category of the biller. e.g. 'electricity', 'airtime', 'cable-tv', 'internet', 'insurance'. Used as a fallback when no exact biller match exists in mapping rules.

float.description: Aggregator or float account details. Used to map transactions to the correct asset account when the operator holds pre-funded float with an aggregator.

aggregator.description: The aggregator whose float was used. e.g. 'vtpass', 'buypower', 'interswitch'.

account_ref.description: The operator's float account reference with this aggregator, if applicable.

balance_before.description: Float balance before this transaction, in the smallest currency unit. Optional but valuable for reconciliation.

balance_after.description: Float balance after this transaction, in the smallest currency unit.

reversal_of.description: If this transaction is a reversal, this field holds the Ledgerise 'id' of the original transaction being reversed. The journal engine uses this to generate a mirror reversal entry.

metadata.description: Adapter-specific and operator-specific key-value pairs. The journal engine does not read this field for mapping decisions, but it is stored with the journal record for auditability. Adapters should include any useful source fields that do not fit the canonical schema.
